name: Linear Combiner on Frozen Base — h' = (1+α)·h + M·T_1 + β
blurb: Non-record submission: 263K-param linear combiner trained by linear regression on cached frozen-base features (h_last, T_1_last) where T_1 is a one-step Coconut-style thinking forward. Combiner is bit-exact identity at zero-init (verified). Trains in <1 sec on cached features. Quantized + brotli-compressed artifact 14.13 MB; total training+quant 8.5 min on 8×H100. Standard sliding-window post-quant val_bpb (base only): 1.26698. Chunked last-position eval (mechanism demo): TRAINED-COMBINER vs IDENTITY-COMBINER post-quant -0.00465 BPB; pre-quant single-rank reference -0.01892 BPB. Standard sliding-window with combiner active at every scored position is computationally infeasible (~5 hours of forward passes), so the submission's leaderboard score equals the base alone. Documented for the mechanism, not the score.

attribution.coconut_thinking_concept: Hao et al. 2024 (Coconut), prior parameter-golf non-record submission 2026-04-30_Coconut_Thinking_Mechanism
